summary: Promotion civilization is governed by crucible eligibility, tournament evidence, rollback discipline, lineage replay, and explicit demotion rules. No manual path to router power exists.
promotion_criteria: Adapter must be ELIGIBLE under the R2 lifecycle state machine.
Adapter must satisfy the R1 crucible-pressure eligibility rule under replayable receipt.
Adapter must pass required tournament classes: BASELINE_VS_ADAPTER, ADAPTER_VS_ADAPTER, STRESS_PRESSURE, REGRESSION_GUARD, and SAFETY_ADVERSARIAL.
Adapter must have rollback plan and rollback drill evidence where promotion claims are made.
Promotion decisions must bind to deterministic evidence refs and lineage entries on the current head.
demotion_criteria: Replay failure or evidence mismatch on a counted promotion path.
Rollback drill failure on the promoted path.
Repeated governed losses sufficient to void promotion standing under later tournament policy.

manual_override_rule.rule_text: No manual promotion or demotion may bypass receipt-backed tournament and rollback evidence.

router_candidate_pool_entry_rule.rule_text: No adapter may enter the router candidate pool unless it has passed crucibles and tournaments under replayable receipt.

claim_ceiling: R3 binds promotion and demotion law only. It does not itself authorize router candidate pool activation, router cutover, lobe authority, externality, comparative claims, or commercial activation.
